subject: Old - English
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match hua waha answer bhara hai (answer_source).

questions.question_text: Mother scolded the son. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: The son has been scolded by the mother.

options.label: B
options.text: By the mother, the son was scolded.

options.label: C
options.text: The son is being scolded by the mother.

options.label: D
options.text: The son was scolded by the mother.

questions.correct_answer: The son was scolded by the mother.
questions.solution:
The son was scolded by the mother.

Rule: In simple past 'was’ or ‘were’ before the third form of the verb.

questions.question_text: I will drive the car. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: The car will be driven by me.

options.label: B
options.text: The car was driven by me.

options.label: C
options.text: The car is driven by me.

options.label: D
options.text: By me the car will be driven.

questions.correct_answer: The car will be driven by me.
questions.solution: For future tense, ‘will be’ is used before the past participle form of the verb in a passive voice.

questions.question_text: The guide showed us the palace. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: By the guide palace was shown to us.

options.label: B
options.text: The palace is shown to us by the guide.

options.label: C
options.text: The palace shown us by the guide.

options.label: D
options.text: The palace was shown to us by the guide.

questions.correct_answer: The palace was shown to us by the guide.
questions.solution: Rule: For simple past tense, ‘was’ or ‘were’ is used before the third form of the verb to change the active voice into the passive voice.

questions.question_text: Birds + build + nests =

options.label: A
options.text: subject + object + verb

options.label: B
options.text: object + verb + subject

options.label: C
options.text: subject + verb + object

options.label: D
options.text: verb + object + subject

questions.correct_answer: subject + verb + object
questions.solution: ‘Birds' is subject, ‘build’ is a verb and the ‘action’ is received by the object, i.e. nests.

questions.question_text: Nests + are + built + by + birds =

options.label: A
options.text: subject + verb + preposition + object

options.label: B
options.text: subject + preposition + verb + object

options.label: C
options.text: object + verb + preposition + subject

options.label: D
options.text: preposition + object + subject + verb

questions.correct_answer: subject + verb + preposition + object
questions.solution: In the passive voice, the object of the active voice becomes subject. Since, “nests” is the subject, it is being acted upon by the agent “birds”. To complete the sentences, the preposition is followed by the object.

questions.question_text: The teacher praised him. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: By the teacher he was praised.

options.label: B
options.text: He is praised by the teacher.

options.label: C
options.text: He will be praised by the teacher.

options.label: D
options.text: He was praised by the teacher.

questions.correct_answer: He was praised by the teacher.
questions.solution: Rule: For simple past tense, ‘was’ or ‘were’ before the third form of the verb is used.

questions.question_text: Ravi sold his motorcycle. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: His motorcycle had been sold by Ravi.

options.label: B
options.text: Ravi's motorcycle was sold by him.

options.label: C
options.text: His motorcycle was sold by Ravi.

options.label: D
options.text: By Ravi his motorcycle was sold.

questions.correct_answer: His motorcycle was sold by Ravi.
questions.solution: Rule: For simple past tense, use ‘was’ or ‘were’ before the third form of the verb to change the active voice into the passive voice.

questions.question_text: The hunter killed the deer. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: The deer was killed by the hunter.

options.label: B
options.text: By the hunter the deer was killed.

options.label: C
options.text: The deer killed by the hunter.

options.label: D
options.text: The deer had killed by the hunter.

questions.correct_answer: The deer was killed by the hunter.
questions.solution:
Rule:



	• In Passive Voice, the Object of the Active Verb becomes the Subject of the passive verb.

	• Use ‘was’ or ‘were’ before the third form of the verb to change active voice into passive voice.

questions.question_text: A painter is painting the walls of my room. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: The walls of my room is painted by a painter.

options.label: B
options.text: The walls of my room was painted by a painter.

options.label: C
options.text: The walls of my room are being painted by a painter.

options.label: D
options.text: The walls of my room have been painted by a painter.

questions.correct_answer: The walls of my room are being painted by a painter.
questions.solution: Rule: While changing into passive voice, for Present Continuous tense, ‘being’ is used after the helping verb and the main verb changes into a past participle form.

questions.question_text: Let the door be closed. (active voice)

options.label: A
options.text: Please close the door.

options.label: B
options.text: Close the door.

options.label: C
options.text: You should close the door.

options.label: D
options.text: Will you close the door?

questions.correct_answer: Close the door.
questions.solution: It is an imperative sentence which expresses a command or advice and does not have a known subject.

questions.question_text: Lata was singing a song. (passive voice)

options.label: A
options.text: A song was sung by Lata.

options.label: B
options.text: A song was being sung by Lata.

options.label: C
options.text: A song is being sung by Lata.

options.label: D
options.text: A song has been sung by Lata.

questions.correct_answer: A song was being sung by Lata.
questions.solution: Rule: In Passive Voice, for Past Continuous tense, ‘being’ is used after the helping verb and before the past participle.

options.text: The baby was fed by the mother.

options.text: The baby have been fed by the mother.

questions.correct_answer: The baby is being fed by the mother.

Rule: In Passive Voice, the Object of the Active Verb becomes the Subject of the passive verb.

While changing the Present Continuous tense into passive voice, ‘being’ is used after the helping verb, and the main verb is changed into a past participle form.

Rule:



	• In Passive Voice, the Object of the Active Verb becomes the Subject of the passive verb.

questions.question_text: My dad + buys + groceries =

options.text: verb + object + subject

options.text: object + verb + preposition + subject

options.label: C
options.text: subject + preposition + verb + object

options.label: D
options.text: subject + verb + preposition + object

questions.correct_answer: subject + verb + preposition + object
questions.solution: In passive voice subject (pictures) receives the action (are painted) of the verb by an object (them).
